Level 1: US1 - Switch between All Chats and Unread view
Title: CLICK Unread filter from All Chats view
Steps:
Precondition: User is on the main chat dashboard in the "All Chats" view with both Read and Unread messages present
1. CLICK **[Unread]** category filter
Expected Result:
- SHOULD transition to the filtered view
- SHOULD display only messages with "Unread" status

Title: CLICK Unread filter with no unread messages
Steps:
Precondition: User is on the main chat dashboard in the "All Chats" view with no Unread messages
1. CLICK **[Unread]** category filter
Expected Result:
- SHOULD transition to the filtered view
- SHOULD display "No unread messages" empty state

Level 2: AC2 - Switching back to All Chats
Title: CLICK All Chats from Unread view
Steps:
Precondition: User is currently viewing the "Unread" filtered list
1. CLICK **[All Chats]** category view
Expected Result:
- SHOULD remove the filter
- SHOULD display all messages (Read and Unread) in chronological order

Title: CLICK All Chats after reading all messages in Unread view
Steps:
Precondition: User is in the "Unread" view and has read all unread messages (now showing empty state)
1. CLICK **[All Chats]** category view
Expected Result:
- SHOULD remove the filter
- SHOULD display all messages (Read and Unread) in chronological order

Level 1: US2 - Unread view filters out read messages
Title: APPLY Unread filter with mixed message statuses
Steps:
Precondition: User has a total of 10 messages (7 Read, 3 Unread)
1. CLICK **[Unread]** filter
Expected Result:
- SHOULD hide the 7 "Read" messages
- SHOULD display only the 3 "Unread" messages in the list

Title: APPLY Unread filter with all read messages
Steps:
Precondition: User has 10 messages, all with "Read" status
1. CLICK **[Unread]** filter
Expected Result:
- SHOULD hide all messages
- SHOULD display "No unread messages" empty state

Title: APPLY Unread filter with all unread messages
Steps:
Precondition: User has 10 messages, all with "Unread" status
1. CLICK **[Unread]** filter
Expected Result: - SHOULD display all 10 "Unread" messages in the list

Level 2: AC2 - Empty state for Unread filter
Title: CLICK Unread filter with zero unread messages
Steps:
Precondition: User has no messages with "Unread" status
1. CLICK **[Unread]** filter
Expected Result: - SHOULD display "No unread messages" empty state or placeholder

Title: CLICK Unread filter with zero total messages
Steps:
Precondition: User has no messages in the system
1. CLICK **[Unread]** filter
Expected Result: - SHOULD display "No unread messages" empty state or placeholder

Level 1: US3 - Message remains visible while reading in Unread view
Level 2: AC1 - Opening an unread message (Sticky View)
Title: CLICK unread message in Unread filter view
Steps:
Precondition: User is in the "Unread" filter view with multiple unread messages
1. CLICK on an unread message (Message A)
Expected Result:
- SHOULD change the status of Message A to "Read"
- SHOULD decrement the Unread Count by 1
- Message A SHOULD remain visible in the current list view

Title: CLICK first unread message when only one exists
Steps:
Precondition: User is in the "Unread" filter view with exactly one unread message
1. CLICK on the unread message (Message A)
Expected Result:
- SHOULD change the status of Message A to "Read"
- SHOULD decrement the Unread Count to 0
- Message A SHOULD remain visible in the current list view

Title: CLICK unread message multiple times
Steps:
Precondition: User is in the "Unread" filter view
1. CLICK on an unread message (Message A)
2. CLICK on Message A again
Expected Result:
- SHOULD keep Message A status as "Read"
- SHOULD NOT decrement the Unread Count again
- Message A SHOULD remain visible in the current list view

Level 2: AC2 - Removing previously read message upon new selection
Title: CLICK second unread message after reading first
Steps:
Precondition: User is in the "Unread" filter view and has just read Message A (which is still visible)
1. CLICK on a different unread message (Message B)
Expected Result:
- Message A SHOULD be immediately removed from the "Unread" list
- Message B SHOULD now become the active "Read" message but remain visible

Title: CLICK third message after reading two messages sequentially
Steps:
Precondition: User is in the "Unread" filter view, has read Message A, then read Message B (only B is visible)
1. CLICK on a different unread message (Message C)
Expected Result:
- Message B SHOULD be immediately removed from the "Unread" list
- Message C SHOULD now become the active "Read" message but remain visible

Title: CLICK unread message when previous read message is still visible
Steps:
Precondition: User is in the "Unread" filter view with Message A (Read, sticky) and Message B (Unread)
1. CLICK on Message B
Expected Result:
- Message A SHOULD be immediately removed from the "Unread" list
- Message B SHOULD change status to "Read" and remain visible

Title: NAVIGATE away and back to Unread view after reading message
Steps:
Precondition: User has read a message in the "Unread" view but it is still visible due to the sticky logic
1. CLICK **[All Chats]**
2. CLICK **[Unread]** filter
Expected Result: - The previously read message SHOULD NOT be visible in the "Unread" list

Title: NAVIGATE to different app section and return to Unread view
Steps:
Precondition: User has read Message A in the "Unread" view (still visible)
1. NAVIGATE to a different section of the app (e.g., Settings)
2. NAVIGATE back to Inbox
3. CLICK **[Unread]** filter
Expected Result: - Message A SHOULD NOT be visible in the "Unread" list

Title: REFRESH page while in Unread view with sticky message
Steps:
Precondition: User has read Message A in the "Unread" view (still visible)
1. REFRESH the page
Expected Result: - Message A SHOULD NOT be visible in the "Unread" list after refresh

Title: NAVIGATE to All Chats and back without reading new messages
Steps:
Precondition: User is in the "Unread" view with Message A (Read, sticky) visible
1. CLICK **[All Chats]**
2. CLICK **[Unread]** filter (without clicking any message in All Chats)
Expected Result: - Message A SHOULD NOT be visible in the "Unread" list
